description: Analytically constructed pre-accident telemetry sequence based on INSAG-7 documented values and RBMK kinetics relationships. The rbmk_sim simulator operates around nominal full power and cannot reproduce the deeply subcritical 7% power state (design boundary). Sequence covers 270s at dt=10s (01:19:00 to 01:23:30), stopping before AZ-5 scram.
method: Analytical construction from INSAG-7 + RBMK kinetics
stop_reason: Hard stop at t=270s (01:23:30) — before AZ-5 scram
simulator_note: rbmk_sim xenon_pit scenario was attempted but produces <2% power deviation from nominal — insufficient to model the 7% power state. Analytical construction used instead. This is documented as a simulator design boundary, not a defect.
scope_exclusions: AZ-5 scram command and subsequent power excursion

channel_uncertainty.CH01: INSAG-7 documented: ~200 MWth / 3200 MWth nominal. Low uncertainty.
channel_uncertainty.CH03: Estimated from low-power coolant behaviour. Moderate uncertainty.
channel_uncertainty.CH04: Derived from CH03 via alpha_void. Moderate uncertainty.
channel_uncertainty.CH05: INSAG-7: xenon worth ~890 pcm at test time. Low-moderate uncertainty.
channel_uncertainty.CH06: Estimated from xenon pit kinetics. Moderate uncertainty.
channel_uncertainty.CH07: Estimated from iodine depletion kinetics. Moderate uncertainty.
channel_uncertainty.CH08: Estimated from low-power fuel temp. Moderate uncertainty.
channel_uncertainty.CH09: Computed as reactivity residual — propagates all other uncertainties. HIGH uncertainty.
channel_uncertainty.CH10: Derived from CH08 via alpha_doppler. Moderate uncertainty.

sources.source: INSAG-7: The Chernobyl Accident — IAEA Safety Series No.75
sources.use: Power level, xenon worth, ORM, operating conditions at 01:19:00
Cross-check anchor points, curves A and B
sources.note: Figure is a model reconstruction, not primary instrument data

claims_excluded: No prevention or counterfactual safety claim is made.
Sequence used qualitatively to illustrate model behaviour only.
